narration: Define a class named Phone with curly braces and a semicolon.
Explain that classes are blueprints and objects are instances of those blueprints.
Define attributes and operations as the two main components of a class.
Enclose class members within curly braces.
Declare attributes such as phone name and size.
Define operations as functions like makeCall and receiveCall.
Specify access specifiers: private, protected, and public.
Set attributes to private and operations to public.
Define a constructor with the same name as the class.
Assign constructor parameters to class attributes.
Create an object of the Phone class by instantiating it with parameters.
Invoke the constructor when creating an object.
Call a public operation on an object using the dot operator.
Instantiate a second object of the same class with different parameters.
Demonstrate that multiple objects can be created from a single class blueprint.
Mention that there are three types of constructors: parametrized, default, and copy.
